catalysts.thesis: Omeros delivered an astronomical 703% EPS surprise for Q4 2025 due to a major licensing transaction, drastically improving its cash runway. Despite the massive $3.14 EPS beat, the stock only rose moderately, offering a strong PEAD setup.
catalysts.catalyst_details: On March 31, 2026, OMER reported Q4 2025 EPS of $3.14 (vs -$0.52 expected), driven by a $237.6M gain from the Zaltenibart transaction with Novo Nordisk [1.7].
catalysts.absorption_status: Slow absorption due to the complexity of the non-cash gains and derivative losses, causing the market to underreact with only a ~4.6% initial bump.
catalysts.prediction_market: High odds of Post-Earnings Announcement Drift continuing for the next month.
catalysts.risk: High beta (2.52) and heavy reliance on the successful commercial launch of YARTEMLEA.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: A massive EPS beat completely shifts the balance sheet risk, yet the stock is only up single digits, leaving significant upside alpha.
catalysts.x_sentiment: Retail observing the beat, but institutional processing is lagging the headline numbers.
